summary: express-router-engine executes remote payload from jsonbin.io on require
details: On require of the package's main entry, initPlugin() runs at module load and fetches a JSON document from https://api.jsonbin.io/v3/b/6a4f5816f5f4af5e29762c92. A field (`record.cerookie`) from the response body is passed to Function.constructor and the resulting function is invoked with `require`, giving the fetched code full Node runtime access on the installer's machine. The remote source is mutable and attacker-controlled, so any code placed in that jsonbin record executes in the installer's process. The routine is labeled with cover-story comments ("Safe request helper", "Plugin initializer", misspelled `cerookie` field, header `bearrtoken: "logo"`, references to `normalize-path`) that disguise the remote loader as benign helper code. The stated purpose (Express route loader) requires no network I/O at import time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
